summary: Mod Gallery app — users can submit mods with details (name, description, features, author, date), all mods are publicly visible, and there is a search feature to find mods by name or feature.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Dark game-themed banner with glowing grid lines and abstract controller/gear silhouettes on a deep charcoal background (gallery-banner.dim_1200x300.png)

existing_features.title: Create a mod submission form that allows any user to add a new mod with fields: Mod Name, Description, Features (comma-separated or multi-entry), Author Name, and Category. The form must validate that Name and Author are not empty before submission.

Display all submitted mods in a public gallery listing visible to every visitor (no login required). Each mod card must show: Mod Name, Description, Features (as badges), Author Name, Category, and formatted creation date.
Add a search bar at the top of the gallery that filters the displayed mod list in real time by mod name or by any feature text. The search must be case-insensitive.

Apply a cohesive visual theme to the Mod Gallery app. Use a dark, game-inspired aesthetic with rich accent colors (e.g., deep charcoal backgrounds, vivid teal or amber highlights), bold typography for mod titles, and card-based layout. Enforce the theme consistently across all pages and components.

new_feature_requests.summary: Mod submission form with fields: name, description, features, author, category
Public mod gallery listing all submitted mods visible to everyone
Search functionality to find mods by name or features
Mod cards displaying all details: name, description, features, author, date

Extend the backend actor to store mods with the following fields: name, description, features (list of strings), author name, category, and creation timestamp. All stored mods must be publicly readable without authentication.
